steps.title: Set the two expressions for y equal and solve for x
steps.content:
• Set the two expressions for $y$ equal: $$3x+13 = 3x^2+13$$
  • Subtract \$13$ from both sides: $$3x = 3x^2$$
  • Divide by \$3$: $$x = x^2$$
  • Rearrange: $$x^2 - x = 0$$
  • Factor: $$x(x-1)=0$$
  • Thus $$x=0$$ or $$x=1$$.

steps.title: Find the corresponding y-values and check the answer choices
steps.content:
• For $$x=0$$, $$y = 3(0)+13 = 13$$ → point $$(0,13)$$
  • For $$x=1$$, $$y = 3(1)+13 = 16$$ → point $$(1,16)$$
  • Among the choices, only $$(0,13)$$ appears, which is choice B.
  • That matches choice B — B is correct.
